sourceShortSummaries: Four newly awakened prisoners break out of their crude cell, arm themselves from the abandoned smithy, and settle on an ambush after overhearing drunk goblin guards nearby.
sourceLongSummaries: Acescale, Gaudin, Loria, and Dilion awaken in a rough prison built inside an abandoned smithy on the grounds of Perdoli Manor in Western Cymea. After introducing themselves and probing the cell, they discover a rotten plank wall, crawl into the workshop, gather improvised tools and gear, overhear drunk goblin guards complaining about Ulgna, Folcan, and Grimstone, and inspect a tidier side room full of scavenged clothing and armor before committing to an attack on the guards.

sourceEntries.title: Waking in the Cell
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The session opens with the four prisoners waking in a crude wooden cell, introducing themselves, finding the rotten plank escape, scavenging tools and clothes from the adjoining rooms, overhearing the drunk goblins beyond the door, and finally settling on a plan to rush the guards from two sides.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Four newly awakened prisoners break out of their crude cell, arm themselves from the abandoned smithy, and settle on an ambush after overhearing drunk goblin guards nearby.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Acescale, Gaudin, Loria, and Dilion awaken in a rough prison built inside an abandoned smithy on the grounds of Perdoli Manor in Western Cymea. After introducing themselves and probing the cell, they discover a rotten plank wall, crawl into the workshop, gather improvised tools and gear, overhear drunk goblin guards complaining about Ulgna, Folcan, and Grimstone, and inspect a tidier side room full of scavenged clothing and armor before committing to an attack on the guards.

locationHints.context: crude prison cell, abandoned smithy, and adjoining storage room
locationHints.notes: The party begins the session imprisoned inside a makeshift cell built within the manor's former workshop.

npcDetails.name: Folcan
npcDetails.context: goblin leader named by the guards
npcDetails.notes: The drunken guards complain that Folcan listens to Ulgna.

npcDetails.name: Ulgna
npcDetails.context: goblin superior blamed by the guards
npcDetails.notes: The guards frame their punishment posting as fallout from Ulgna and her allies drawing Grimstone's ire.

npcDetails.name: Grimstone
npcDetails.notes: The guards say the prisoners are being kept for Grimstone.

npcDetails.name: Morky
npcDetails.context: drunk goblin guard at the card game
npcDetails.notes: One of the unseen guards accuses Morky of cheating.

itemDetails.name: smith's tools
itemDetails.notes: Dilion scavenges smith's tools from the abandoned workshop.

itemDetails.name: tinker's tools
itemDetails.notes: Acescale finds usable tinker's tools among the workshop debris.

itemDetails.name: magical formal outfit
itemDetails.notes: Acescale detects transmutation magic on an old but pristine gentleman's dinner outfit in the side room.

sourceShortSummaries: The party's clumsy two-door ambush still succeeds, and the three drunken jailer goblins are killed in the forge-side guard room.
With the first guards dead, the party loots back their gear, rests in the smithy, and surveys the grounds and nearby barracks of Perdoli Manor before choosing a stealthier next move.
The party tries to bluff the barracks with Gaudin disguised as Morky and Loria posing as a captive, but Folcan reads the scene too quickly for the ruse to hold.
When the goblins move to grab Loria, she drops a fog cloud over the path, and the party wins the skirmish fast enough to drag Folcan back alive before the barracks reacts.
Back at the smithy, the party breaks Folcan's nerve and learns how Grimstone's prisoner racket and tunnel routes into the manor work.
sourceLongSummaries: After discovering one of the two doors into the goblin room is barred, the party improvises a distraction and bursts into the first fight of the session. The goblin jailers are drunk but armed, and the brawl turns messy with darkness, acid, arrows, and Loria's brutal work at the knees, yet the group keeps the noise contained and kills the three guards before the rest of the manor notices.

Once the immediate danger passes, the group searches the goblin room and recovered stores, reclaiming weapons, cards, food, and scattered personal gear. They step outside long enough to understand that they are on the edge of sprawling Perdoli Manor, with gardens, hedges, a distant main villa, and a nearby barracks, then settle in for a short rest, a level-up pause, and a longer planning discussion about whether to leave, hide, or probe the goblins further.

Instead of storming the barracks, the party stages a fake prisoner transfer on the hedgerow path: Gaudin disguises himself as Morky, Loria pretends to be bound, and the others wait behind cover while Loria telepathically feeds Gaudin Goblin lines. The act is awkward but almost good enough to draw out two guards and then Folcan, yet once one goblin spots Loria's hidden sword and Folcan arrives, he sends a warning to Ulgna, blasts Gaudin with his toxic staff, and orders Loria seized, leaving the bluff on the verge of collapse.

Once Folcan orders Loria grabbed and sends one goblin running with instructions for Ulgna, Loria answers by filling the path and hedgerow with fog. Fighting through the obscuring mist, she cuts down the goblin holding her, Gaudin slips clear and fires from range, Dilion and the steel defender press from behind the hedge, and Acescale drops another guard with his crossbow. The last path goblin is knocked unconscious, Folcan is taken alive, and the party retreats to the smithy with just enough time to question him before the barracks comes looking.

Back at the smithy, the party revives Folcan, takes away his bone staff, and alternates threats, acid, and questions until he stops posturing. He explains that Grimstone appears in black robes and a white marble mask, takes one prisoner each week into the manor, and pays the goblins in loot while keeping the best treasure. He also reveals that ten goblins plus Ulgna remain in the barracks, that hobgoblins guard the outer gates and the manor door, and that tunnels from the barracks and kitchen, with a rumored chapel entrance, all lead toward the wine cellar, leaving the party leaning toward a tunnel approach and using Folcan to buy time.

sourceEntries.title: The Drunk Goblins Fall
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Once the ambush starts, the whole scene stays focused on the first guard-room fight: the party fumbles the doorway plan, rolls into initiative, and then steadily cuts down the three drunk goblin jailers in the forge-side room before the magical darkness and the fight both end.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party's clumsy two-door ambush still succeeds, and the three drunken jailer goblins are killed in the forge-side guard room.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After discovering one of the two doors into the goblin room is barred, the party improvises a distraction and bursts into the first fight of the session. The goblin jailers are drunk but armed, and the brawl turns messy with darkness, acid, arrows, and Loria's brutal work at the knees, yet the group keeps the noise contained and kills the three guards before the rest of the manor notices.

locationHints.context: guard room beside the smithy prison
locationHints.notes: The fight happens in the adjoining room where the goblin guards had been drinking and gambling.

npcDetails.name: Morky
npcDetails.notes: One of the three jailer goblins killed in the fight.

npcDetails.name: Norrica
npcDetails.notes: One of the three jailer goblins defeated in the guard room.

npcDetails.name: Skelver
npcDetails.notes: One of the three jailer goblins defeated in the guard room.

itemDetails.name: Loria's short swords
itemDetails.notes: The goblin guards had been carrying Loria's stolen weapons.

mainEnemies.name: Morky, Norrica, and Skelver
mainEnemies.notes: Three drunk goblin jailers guarding the forge-side prison room.

combatHints.notes: The party springs the ambush in the guard room and wipes out the three prison guards before the wider estate is alerted.

sourceEntries.title: Breather on the Manor Grounds
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: With the guard room secured, the party loots weapons and stolen gear, steps outside to understand the ruined manor grounds around them, recovers equipment and food, takes a short rest and level-up pause, and then surveys the nearby barracks and overgrown estate while deciding what kind of approach to try next.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: With the first guards dead, the party loots back their gear, rests in the smithy, and surveys the grounds and nearby barracks of Perdoli Manor before choosing a stealthier next move.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Once the immediate danger passes, the group searches the goblin room and recovered stores, reclaiming weapons, cards, food, and scattered personal gear. They step outside long enough to understand that they are on the edge of sprawling Perdoli Manor, with gardens, hedges, a distant main villa, and a nearby barracks, then settle in for a short rest, a level-up pause, and a longer planning discussion about whether to leave, hide, or probe the goblins further.

locationHints.context: smithy, gear room, and overgrown grounds outside the prison building
locationHints.notes: The beat expands the party's understanding of the manor layout while they recover and plan.

npcDetails.name: Grimstone
npcDetails.notes: The group's recovered information and discussion reinforce that the prisoners are being held for Grimstone inside the manor.

itemDetails.name: Acescale's playing cards
itemDetails.notes: The cards taken by the goblins are recovered from the guard room.

itemDetails.name: silver ring with weathered pearl
itemDetails.notes: Gaudin reclaims a silver ring set with a pearl that had been glowing faintly.

itemDetails.name: stolen rations and brandy
itemDetails.notes: The goblin guard room contains scavenged food, cheap apple brandy, and picked-over prisoner supplies.

sourceEntries.title: A Goblin Ruse Unravels
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The next phase is entirely about the deception attempt: the party decides to use Loria's Goblin and silent speech with Gaudin's disguise, stages a fake prisoner transfer outside the barracks, and almost sells the act before Folcan and the other goblins grow suspicious and Loria breaks the standoff with a fog cloud.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party tries to bluff the barracks with Gaudin disguised as Morky and Loria posing as a captive, but Folcan reads the scene too quickly for the ruse to hold.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Instead of storming the barracks, the party stages a fake prisoner transfer on the hedgerow path: Gaudin disguises himself as Morky, Loria pretends to be bound, and the others wait behind cover while Loria telepathically feeds Gaudin Goblin lines. The act is awkward but almost good enough to draw out two guards and then Folcan, yet once one goblin spots Loria's hidden sword and Folcan arrives, he sends a warning to Ulgna, blasts Gaudin with his toxic staff, and orders Loria seized, leaving the bluff on the verge of collapse.

locationHints.context: hedgerow path between the smithy and the nearby barracks
locationHints.notes: The party tries to exploit cover near the barracks rather than storming the building directly.

npcDetails.name: Folcan
npcDetails.notes: Folcan ignores the cover story, warns Ulgna to send help if he does not return, blasts Gaudin with his staff, and orders Loria seized.

npcDetails.name: Ulgna
npcDetails.context: superior goblin leader back in the barracks
npcDetails.notes: Folcan orders that Ulgna be told to send help if he does not return.

npcDetails.name: Morky
npcDetails.context: drunk goblin whose identity Gaudin imitates
npcDetails.notes: Gaudin poses as Morky to sell the fake prisoner transfer, and Folcan uses Morky's drunkenness as the excuse he sends back to Ulgna.

sourceEntries.title: Fog Fight by the Barracks
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Once the fog cloud goes up, the deception is over and the whole beat becomes a tight skirmish outside the barracks, with the party using the hedgerow and obscuring mist to kill the goblin guards quickly enough to avoid drawing the full barracks, then dragging the surviving boss back for questioning.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: When the goblins move to grab Loria, she drops a fog cloud over the path, and the party wins the skirmish fast enough to drag Folcan back alive before the barracks reacts.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Once Folcan orders Loria grabbed and sends one goblin running with instructions for Ulgna, Loria answers by filling the path and hedgerow with fog. Fighting through the obscuring mist, she cuts down the goblin holding her, Gaudin slips clear and fires from range, Dilion and the steel defender press from behind the hedge, and Acescale drops another guard with his crossbow. The last path goblin is knocked unconscious, Folcan is taken alive, and the party retreats to the smithy with just enough time to question him before the barracks comes looking.

locationHints.context: fog-shrouded path and hedgerow outside the barracks
locationHints.notes: The fight stays outside the barracks itself and ends quickly enough to avoid a full alarm.

npcDetails.name: Folcan
npcDetails.notes: Folcan survives the path fight, is taken captive, and is dragged back to the smithy for interrogation.

mainEnemies.name: Folcan and three barracks goblins
mainEnemies.notes: The fight on the path involves Folcan, the suspicious goblin already on scene, and the two warriors who come out with him from the barracks.

combatHints.notes: Loria's fog cloud and the hedgerow let the party isolate the path fight, finish it inside roughly three rounds, and retreat with Folcan before the full barracks reacts.

sourceEntries.title: Folcan Names the Tunnels
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Back at the forge and hedge line, the party spends the rest of the session interrogating Folcan, learning how Grimstone's prisoner racket works, how many goblins and hobgoblins are nearby, and where the tunnel entrances might reach the wine cellar, before ending on a tentative plan to send Folcan back and exploit the tunnels next time.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Back at the smithy, the party breaks Folcan's nerve and learns how Grimstone's prisoner racket and tunnel routes into the manor work.

He explains that Grimstone appears in black robes and a white marble mask, takes one prisoner each week into the manor, and pays the goblins in loot while keeping the best treasure. He also reveals that ten goblins plus Ulgna remain in the barracks, that hobgoblins guard the outer gates and the manor door, and that tunnels from the barracks and kitchen, with a rumored chapel entrance, all lead toward the wine cellar, leaving the party leaning toward a tunnel approach and using Folcan to buy time.

locationHints.context: smithy and roadside cover near the barracks
locationHints.notes: The party questions Folcan back near the prison building after the second fight.

npcDetails.name: Folcan
npcDetails.notes: After being revived and threatened, Folcan explains Grimstone's arrangement, the remaining goblin numbers, the hobgoblin guards, and the tunnel entrances.

npcDetails.name: Grimstone
npcDetails.notes: Folcan describes Grimstone as a figure in black robes with a white marble mask who takes prisoners into the manor.

npcDetails.notes: Folcan complains about Ulgna while describing the goblin hierarchy and the surviving force in the barracks.
